subject:
Re: Why is the MCore reconstruction "much" better than
 relion_reconstruct?
body:
Hi Alister,

We need to do 3D classification in Relion (or any other program that can do 
it) and the goal is to use the best possible input for this classification. 
The above was with WarpTools ts_export_particles --3d and 
relion_reconstruct. I'm trying now WarpTools ts_export_particles --2d 
and relion_tomo_reconstruct_particle. I will report back how this behaves.

I suspect it's related how missing information in Fourier space is handled. 
For instance, if I make an MCore reconstruction with I2 symmetry imposed, 
the resolution is great. If I make the same reconstruction in MCore with C1 
symmetry imposed and then real space average the resulting map with I2 
symmetry, the resolution is terrible. This makes me think that in MCore, 
when you impose symmetry, only defined regions in reciprocal space are 
include in the symmetrization. Maybe the same is happening when summing up 
all particles and the information of what is measured and missing in 
reciprocal space is lost when exporting the 3D particles for Relion (the 3D 
CTF volumes show it, but I'm not sure how it is handled in Relion).

Best, Simon

On Tuesday, November 26, 2024 at 3:52:31 PM UTC-5 alist...@gmail.com wrote:

> Hey Simon,
>
> That is a big effect - the first thing that came to mind was that there is 
> an extra interpolation in your relion_reconstruct workflow, you first 
> reconstruct 3D particles in Warp then rotate and average a bunch of those 
> in RELION... this would affect the two halves in the same way and so 
> shouldn't have an effect on the FSC though.
>
> I guess I'm not sure why you feel the need to reproduce the reconstruction 
> outside of M... what is the benefit of being able to produce the same 
> result with relion_reconstruct?
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
>
> On Tue, 26 Nov 2024 at 11:58, Simon Jenni <je...@crystal.harvard.edu> 
> wrote:
>
>> I suspect there must be other factors at play. I don't think the observed 
>> difference in map quality can be just accounted by
>> - temporal resolution of particle poses
>> - per-image/per-tilt series weights
>> Is the difference coming from how missing information in Fourier space is 
>> treated?
>>
>> [image: Screenshot 2024-11-26 at 2.54.04 PM.png]
>>
>>
>> On Wednesday, November 20, 2024 at 9:01:15 AM UTC-5 Simon Jenni wrote:
>>
>>> Hi Juha, I calculated the FSCs from the half maps by myself with the 
>>> same mask.
>>> Best, Simon
>>> On Wednesday, November 20, 2024 at 2:21:34 AM UTC-5 juha.hu...@gmail.com 
>>> wrote:
>>>
>>>> Hi Simon,
>>>>
>>>> Does the mask for relion_postprocess cover roughly the same area as 
>>>> what is being used in Mcore?
>>>>
>>>> Cheers,
>>>> Juha 
>>>>
>>>> On Monday, 18 November 2024 at 21:03:33 UTC je...@crystal.harvard.edu 
>>>> wrote:
>>>>
>>>>> I'm getting the same result with and without --3d_rot. Not sure what 
>>>>> this is actually supposed to do in relion_reconstruct. Maybe ignored when 
>>>>> input is 3D particles?
>>>>>
>>>>> /programs/x86_64-linux/relion/4.0.1_cu11.6/bin/relion_reconstruct | 
>>>>> grep 3d_rot
>>>>>                    --3d_rot (false) : Perform 3D rotations instead of 
>>>>> backprojections from 2D images
>>>>>
>>>>> On Monday, November 18, 2024 at 2:10:10 PM UTC-5 Simon Jenni wrote:
>>>>>
>>>>>> Sorry, the missing --ctf was when I edited the email. It was used to 
>>>>>> reconstruct the half map2.
>>>>>> The maps look reasonable even without --3d_rot, I will re-run with 
>>>>>> --3d_rot.
>>>>>> Simon
>>>>>>
>>>>>>
>>>>>>
>>>>>> On Monday, November 18, 2024 at 1:56:32 PM UTC-5 alist...@gmail.com 
>>>>>> wrote:
>>>>>>
>>>>>>> it also looks like you didn't apply the ctf to your second subset...
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> On Mon, 18 Nov 2024 at 10:55, Alister Burt <alist...@gmail.com> 
>>>>>>> wrote:
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> and yes, --ctf is correct
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> On Mon, 18 Nov 2024 at 10:55, Alister Burt <alist...@gmail.com> 
>>>>>>>> wrote:
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>> Hi Simon,
>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>> Broadly yes, the M reconstruction is doing a few things that are 
>>>>>>>>> not taken into account by the Warp particle export tool
>>>>>>>>> - temporal resolution of particle poses
>>>>>>>>> - per-image/per-tilt series weights
>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>> You should also add `--3d_rot` to your relion_reconstruct command 
>>>>>>>>> - do your maps look reasonable without it?
>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>> Cheers,
>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>> Alister
>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>> On Mon, 18 Nov 2024 at 10:13, Simon Jenni <
>>>>>>>>> je...@crystal.harvard.edu> wrote:
>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> My MCore reconstruction is at 3.47 A. I then export 3D particles 
>>>>>>>>>> and CTF volume and use relion_reconstruct to calculate half maps (no 
>>>>>>>>>> refinement). The resolution drops to 4.18. Is this expected, because 
>>>>>>>>>> WarpTools ts_export_particles is not exporting everything of the MCore 
>>>>>>>>>> optimization? Or is there some weighting in the MCore reconstruction that 
>>>>>>>>>> cannot be exported? Is just --ctf the correct relion_reconstruct option in 
>>>>>>>>>> this situation?
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> Thanks, Simon
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> MCore reconstruction, C1 symmetry
>>>>>>>>>> resolution (from MCore half maps) = 3.47
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> export 3D particles and 3D CTF with WarpTools ts_export_particles
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> relion_reconstruct --i partilces.star --subset 1 \
>>>>>>>>>> --o rec_half_map1.mrc \
>>>>>>>>>> --sym C1 \
>>>>>>>>>> --ctf --pad 2
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> relion_reconstruct --i partilces.star --subset 2 \
>>>>>>>>>> --o rec_half_map2.mrc \
>>>>>>>>>> --sym C1 \
>>>>>>>>>>  --pad 2
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> resolution (from relion_reconstruct half maps) = 4.18
>>>>>>>>>>
